title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The sharing economy is a term used to describe platforms that enable individuals to share underutilised assets with others, a model (0) which has reshaped consumer behaviour across a range of industries. Transport, accommodation and professional services have all been transformed. (9) Although the sharing economy was initially associated with principles of collaborative consumption and community benefit, commercial considerations have come to dominate most of the major platforms. (10) Such has been the growth of ride-hailing and home-rental services that traditional taxi and hotel industries have faced significant structural disruption. These platforms generate substantial economic activity, and (11) Provided that users are willing to accept the terms of service, access to cars, accommodation and everyday tools can be obtained rapidly and conveniently. Not (12) only did the sharing economy create new income opportunities for asset owners, but it also significantly complicated existing frameworks of employment law and consumer protection. (13) Regardless of the regulatory challenges involved, governments in most countries have struggled to keep pace with the speed at which these platforms have scaled globally. Not (14) until a series of high-profile legal disputes drew public attention to the conditions of platform workers did legislators begin to address the employment status of gig economy participants in any meaningful way. (15) After years of prioritising growth over governance, many platforms are now engaging more seriously with questions of worker protection and environmental sustainability. Whether these adjustments amount to genuine reform remains a matter of debate. (16) Whether the sharing economy ultimately delivers net social benefit or deepens inequality will depend significantly on the regulatory choices that societies make in the years ahead.

example.text: The sharing economy is a term used to describe platforms that enable individuals to share underutilised assets with others, a model (0) which has reshaped consumer behaviour across a range of industries.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Temporal preposition introducing a phrase indicating that a period has elapsed before the action in the main clause.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
